pattern: A monotonic increasing stack of bar indices lets each bar be popped exactly once to compute the widest rectangle it can cap, giving a linear-time maximum.
hints: Every maximal rectangle has a height equal to some bar; the question is how far left and right that bar can extend before a shorter bar stops it.
Maintaining a stack of bars in increasing height order means that when a shorter bar arrives, the bars it ends can be measured immediately.

Push indices while heights rise; when a bar is lower than the stack top, pop it, use the popped height with the width spanning back to the new top, and track the best area, sentinel-ending with a zero-height bar.

stress.description: random heights with some zeros to break the histogram
stress.note: zeros and equal runs stress the stack popping
